steps.title: Find the key signal
steps.content: The decisive clue is the misconception at the heart of the naming: explorers believed the peninsula to be an island and therefore named it after the fictional island.

steps.title: Apply the signal to the choices
steps.content: Choice D states the misconception explicitly—”Thinking it was an island”—and shows how it directly caused the naming of the peninsula after the fictional island. The other three either skip the misconception (A, C) or mention the source without explaining the false belief that motivated the naming (B). That matches choice D — D is correct.
